title: chionolaena seemannii
intro: Chionolaena seemannii, also known as Hawaiian tree fern or ko'oko'olau, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Chionolaena seemannii include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Chionolaena seemannii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or topical creams for its medicinal uses.
